north_star_verdict.headline: Zycus is the confirmed front-runner in active contracting, but the 31 Jul close is not credible: legal has MSA/OF/SOW backlogged (a legal rep departed), the SOW scoping deep-dive is not yet run, updated pricing and privacy answers are still owed, and Jason Chan's budget sign-off is not in writing.
north_star_verdict.math: Close 31 Jul 2026, roughly 25 days out. Created 17 Oct 2025 (262 days old). Stage Shortlisted but the deal is running a contracting motion (InfoSec + AP completed 8 Jun, Legal/CLM deep-dive 18 Jun, Implementation Readiness 9 Jul). CloseDate was pushed 30 Jun -> 31 Jul on 25 Jun, the second slip after 31 May -> 30 Jun on 27 May. Remaining path: privacy sub-processor/AI answers (this week), updated pricing + Certinal signature pricing (1 week), SOW scoping + finalize PS cost (1-2 weeks), legal redlines with a backlogged team ('a few weeks' per Abe 25 Jun), signature (1 week) = 4-5 weeks minimum against 3.6 weeks remaining. Realistic signature: late Aug 2026. Deal is healthy and engaged; the sole problem is the date.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Upside Key Deal (date slips to Aug 2026)
evidence: Abe Comarow confirmed 'we are the front runner and need to continue hitting the mark' (Next Step, 25 Jun 2026).
CloseDate pushed twice by Karson Keogh: 31 May -> 30 Jun (27 May) then 30 Jun -> 31 Jul (25 Jun), signalling the date is a moving placeholder, not a buyer-committed signature date (field history).
Legal has MSA, Order Form and draft SOW in review since 20 May, backlogged and 'impacted by a recent legal rep leaving... should take a few weeks' (Next Step, 25 Jun 2026).
SOW scoping deep-dive to scope roles/resources for accurate PS costs was only being 'set up' as of 25 Jun; the Implementation Readiness session ran 9 Jul to refine it (event 9 Jul 2026).
Privacy team raised a sub-processor list request and Merlin AI training-data questions on 1 Jul; Abe said 2 Jul 'we have what we need for now other than these' (Task, 2 Jul 2026).
Coupa and Zip 'still in running due to dropping their costs significantly which could be a slight risk'; Abe requested updated list-vs-CC pricing to defend internally (Next Step, 25 Jun 2026).
No written budget/signature sign-off from Economic Buyer Jason Chan (Controller) is logged, though he asked to join all implementation meetings going forward (email, 24 Jun 2026).

metrics.narrative: The value case is procurement-transformation and cycle-time driven: replacing a NetSuite procurement module Abe Comarow (Senior Strategic Sourcing Manager) deemed unfit, to gain spend visibility, contract control and AP automation across a full source-to-pay scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin Intake per Products__c). A business-case readout to finance ran 8 May 2026 (Next Step 28 Apr: 'CC business case readout with CFO May 8') showing cycle-time reduction and savings (echoed in AIS_Why). On the 5 Jun InfoSec call, Michael welcomed the user-based, module pricing as 'far more transparent' than consumption billing, reinforcing a defensible cost story. No single verbatim savings figure is quoted by the buyer in the transcripts read, so the quantified ROI is corroborated by the readout artifact rather than a buyer quote; re-confirming the accepted savings number in writing with Jason Chan is the move.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is Jason Chan, Controller (OpportunityContactRole), who received the 8 May business-case readout and, per Abe's 24 Jun email, 'told me he wants to be in the implementation meetings going forward' - a direct signal the budget owner is now engaging personally rather than passively. Abe Comarow controls the commercial thread day-to-day (requested list-vs-CC pricing 25 Jun, envelope counts for signature pricing 18 Jun), but final budget authority sits with Jason and, above him, finance. Jason's rising attendance is positive; the gap is that no written budget or signature sign-off from him is logged. The move is to convert his implementation-meeting presence into an explicit budget/timeline commitment before the close date.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are explicit from the deep-dives. Legal (18 Jun CLM) tested AI-assisted authoring, fallback-clause libraries, Instareview risk scoring, parallel Word redlining without check-in/check-out locking (Abe: 'is there a way that we don't lock the document... clearly redline'), click-through/online-terms ingestion (Joseph Reiber), and a future Claude/LLM integration with tokenization-cost transparency (Abe). Procurement/IT (5 Jun) prioritized supplier onboarding (iSupplier), supplier risk (iRisk vs incumbent Upguard), NetSuite integration lift, and transparent, non-consumption pricing (Michael). Signature handling is a criterion in its own right: internal HR volume must stay on Adobe Sign (30k, committed to 10/8/2028) while Zycus covers procurement signatures. Fit is strong on classical CLM/P2P; the exposed criterion is AI/agentic depth (see competition and AI-fit).
sources: Avoma CLM call 18 Jun 2026 (Abe, Joseph Reiber, Walter)
Avoma InfoSec call 5 Jun 2026 (Michael, Colin Taira)

decision_process.narrative: The call sequence maps a committee moving from evaluation into contracting: business case to finance (8 May) -> AP/Finance + InfoSec/IT-Asset-Management demos (5 Jun) -> Legal/CLM deep-dive (18 Jun, adding new counsel Joseph Reiber and Privacy VP) -> Implementation Readiness session (9 Jul, with Jason Chan asking to join). Approvals run in parallel across procurement (Abe, Colin Taira, Garry Hill), legal (Bobbie Reyes GC, Joseph Reiber, Kane Christianson), InfoSec/GRC (Marques Johnson), AP (Heather Williams) and finance (Jason Chan). The signature choke point is the SOW (roles/resources still being scoped) plus legal redlines on a backlogged team; MSA + Order Form are progressing. The process is buyer-led and healthy, the risk is throughput, not direction.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting is live: Abe requested MSA, Order Form and draft SOW for legal review on 20 May; legal has held them since, backlogged and slowed by a departed legal rep ('should take a few weeks' - Abe, 25 Jun). InfoSec and AP sessions completed 8 Jun. The SOW is the predictor and remains open - roles/resources being scoped via the 9 Jul Implementation Readiness session before PS cost can finalize. Signature mechanics were discussed on 18 Jun: Zycus offers native Adobe Sign and DocuSign integration plus its own e-signature, but Consumer Cellular must retain Adobe Sign for internal HR volume (committed to 10/8/2028). Open paper items: finalized SOW scope, legal redlines back from counsel, and the privacy team's sub-processor/DPA and Merlin AI training questions (raised 1 Jul). As a US deal, no PO is required to invoice - do not treat a missing PO as a gap.

identify_pain.narrative: Confirmed operational pain: the incumbent NetSuite procurement module is unfit (missing contract hierarchy, weak approval flows, AP limitations), leaving fragmented tooling, poor spend visibility and manual contract handling. On 18 Jun Walter voiced a concrete legal pain: mandatory-clause libraries in prior tools became stale and untrusted ('you tell me this is a mandatory clause. But the last 15 contracts you've done, you haven't included it'), and the manual step of uploading signed contracts off the desktop ('I'm somewhat behind right now'). Lisa (Privacy) raised DPA-modification tracking and post-termination data-destruction as unmet needs. Pain owners: Abe (procurement fragmentation), Walter/Joseph (contract lifecycle burden), Garry Hill (P2P/PO-to-contract linkage). Pain is well-articulated and current.

champion.narrative: Abe Comarow (Senior Strategic Sourcing Manager) is a strong, active champion: he drove the business-case readout, coordinated every validation session (InfoSec, AP, Legal, Implementation Readiness), submitted the MSA/OF/SOW to his own legal team, confirmed 'we are the front runner' (25 Jun) and is orchestrating pricing and SOW next steps. His advocacy is concrete - he pulls Zycus into internal scheduling and defends the choice while asking for pricing ammunition against Coupa/Zip. Last contact 2 Jul 2026 (privacy-questions email). A supporting coach is Colin Taira (Director of Procurement), engaged and positive since 5 Jun. The only champion risk is load: Abe is single-threading the commercial defence against two rivals cutting price, so reinforcing him with a total-cost story and a direct Jason Chan touch protects him.

competition.narrative: Zycus is the front-runner in a narrowing field. The live threat is price: Coupa and Zip 'dropped their costs significantly' and remain in the running (Abe, 25 Jun), pressuring Abe to justify Zycus internally with list-vs-CC pricing. SAP Ariba sits on Competitors__c but has no active 2026 buyer evidence (faded). NetSuite is the incumbent being displaced (deemed unfit). Adobe Sign is a signature-only incumbent that must be retained for internal HR volume. We win on classical CLM/P2P depth (parallel Word redlining, clause/fallback libraries, Instareview, NetSuite integration, PO-to-contract linkage) and transparent user-based pricing (Michael called it 'far more transparent', 5 Jun); the wedge against price-cutters is total cost of ownership plus the CLM/search-genie and NetSuite-integration proof they cannot match. The exposed flank is AI/agentic: AIS is Negative and the buyer showed little agentic appetite, so do not lead with Merlin.

deal_movement.summary: The deal accelerated into contracting through June and is engaged today: InfoSec/AP completed, a strong Legal/CLM deep-dive, the Economic Buyer asking to join implementation meetings, and an Implementation Readiness session on 9 Jul. The date, not the direction, is the issue - CloseDate has been pushed twice and legal is backlogged.

Legal/CLM deep-dive: strong engagement on AI authoring, fallback clauses, click-through terms and signature module; Certinal signature pricing and Claude/LLM integration raised

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is the front-runner in a two-to-three-vendor field, but Coupa and Zip are the live current threat: both 'dropped their costs significantly' and remain in the running as of 25 Jun 2026, forcing Abe to defend Zycus pricing internally. NetSuite is the incumbent being displaced (unfit for purpose); SAP Ariba is on the CRM field with no active 2026 evidence (faded); Adobe Sign is a signature-only incumbent Consumer Cellular will retain for internal HR volume. Win path: total-cost-of-ownership pricing plus CLM/search-genie and NetSuite-integration proof the price-cutters cannot match.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Coupa and zip still in running due to dropping their costs significantly which could be a slight risk
competitors.how_we_win: Frame list-vs-CC pricing on total cost of ownership (3-yr SaaS + PS + integration) vs Coupa's lower headline SaaS but higher change cost; anchor on the accepted savings case.

competitors.name: Zip
competitors.quote: Coupa and zip still in running due to dropping their costs significantly which could be a slight risk
competitors.how_we_win: Out-scope Zip on true CLM authoring, supplier risk (iRisk) and NetSuite PO-to-contract linkage that an intake-first tool cannot deliver.

competitors.name: NetSuite Procurement (incumbent being displaced)
competitors.quote: NetSuite procurement module unfit for purpose: missing contract hierarchy, limited approval flows, AP challenges
competitors.how_we_win: Prove the live NetSuite integration (recorded PO/GR/invoice sync video Abe requested) so replacement feels low-risk before the Spring 2027 renewal.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.how_we_win: No active 2026 buyer signal; keep the transparent user-based pricing contrast ready if Ariba resurfaces.

competitors.name: Adobe Sign (signature incumbent)
competitors.quote: we have 30k now with AdobeSign. We are also committed on that order until 10/8/2028
competitors.how_we_win: Do not fight the internal-HR volume; position Zycus e-signature only for procurement contracts via native Adobe Sign/DocuSign integration and price the envelope split accordingly.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / status quo
competitors.quote: Expressed comfort with the current situation and acknowledged the need for better visibility into spending
competitors.how_we_win: The NetSuite renewal (Spring 2027) and a departed legal rep make inertia costly; hold the mutual close plan to the implementation runway.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit on classical CLM/P2P and pricing transparency; exposed on AI/agentic depth and on signatures where Adobe Sign must be retained for internal HR volume.

best_practice_check.summary: Front-runner in contracting; the win now hinges on locking the EB in writing, neutralizing the Coupa/Zip price cut, and resetting the date to a credible plan.
flags: Convert Jason Chan's (Controller) new implementation-meeting attendance into a written budget and signature commitment before 31 Jul.
Neutralize Coupa/Zip price cuts with a total-cost-of-ownership package: list-vs-CC pricing plus the per-envelope signature quote in one view for Abe.
De-risk the date with a mutual close plan sequencing privacy answers, legal redlines and SOW scope to a realistic late-Aug signature.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AIS is Negative (2.0): the 8 Jan exec session produced 16 action items, none touching AI/Merlin/Agentic, and the account is slow to adopt AI. Legal did show narrow curiosity on 18 Jun (Claude/LLM integration, tokenization cost) but those threads are unresolved. The deal is won on classical CLM/P2P value, not agentic - do not over-index the Merlin story.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 2.0, Status Negative. Across 11+ discovered calls no Consumer Cellular stakeholder requested an AI demo block; the exec session's 16 action items are all classical procurement (pricing, ROI, eProcurement, CLM). The only AI pull is legal's interest in a future Claude/LLM authoring integration and its token-cost transparency (18 Jun), which Zycus still owes an answer on. Read as AI Resistant with a thin legal-integration curiosity, not agentic appetite.

items.detail: 31 Jul close not credible: legal redlines backlogged (departed rep), SOW scope not finalized, pricing and privacy answers owed; CloseDate already pushed twice.
Coupa and Zip dropped costs significantly and remain in the running; Abe needs updated list-vs-CC pricing to defend Zycus internally.

Economic Buyer Jason Chan is engaging but has given no written budget/signature commitment.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is the confirmed front-runner in active contracting with a strong CPO-adjacent champion in Abe and the budget owner Jason Chan now joining implementation meetings; the only things between us and the win are a compressed 31 Jul date on a backlogged legal team and Coupa/Zip cutting price.

win_position.text: Held at a Shortlisted-tier score near 72: fit and champion earn more, but we cap it below Vendor Selected because the award is not yet contractually locked and two rivals just cut price.
Abe Comarow confirmed 'we are the front runner and need to continue hitting the mark' (25 Jun) while legal holds the MSA, Order Form and draft SOW - buyer behaviour consistent with a chosen vendor.
Coupa and Zip 'dropped their costs significantly' and remain in the running (25 Jun), so a price-only reopening is the main way this slips backward if we do not land total cost of ownership.
Economic Buyer Jason Chan is engaging but has given no written budget or signature commitment, leaving the number exposed to late renegotiation.

deal_momentum.text: Seniority is rising: Controller Jason Chan asked to join all implementation meetings (24 Jun) and a Privacy VP joined the 18 Jun CLM deep-dive.
Commercial and legal tracks are all live in the last 30 days: InfoSec/AP completed 8 Jun, CLM deep-dive 18 Jun, Implementation Readiness 9 Jul.
Momentum is throughput-limited: CloseDate was pushed twice (31 May -> 30 Jun -> 31 Jul) and legal is backlogged after a rep departure.

customer_commitment.text: Buyer-initiated the 'Finalizing Agreements' scheduling thread and requested updated pricing, signature pricing and a SOW deep-dive - all pull, not push.
Full committee engaged (finance, legal, InfoSec/GRC, AP, IT, procurement), and Adobe Sign envelope commitments were shared to scope signature pricing.
No signed SOW or written EB sign-off yet; commitment is strong behaviourally but not yet on paper.

deal_risk.text: The 31 Jul date is not credible against 4-5 weeks of remaining legal, SOW, pricing and privacy work on a backlogged legal team.
Price pressure from Coupa/Zip could reopen the commercials if the total-cost case is not delivered to Abe this week.
AIS is Negative and agentic appetite is thin, so the Merlin/AI upsell narrative should not be leaned on to justify price.
